ops.title: ccx web outline/read/search ‚Äî architecture, the v0.9.1 challenge-detection contract, and what remains unverified

ops.body:
`ccx web` (outline/read/search) shipped 2026-07-08: in-process engine in internal/web (OpEdit-style dispatch special-case, NOT a router backend), fetch cascade jina‚Üíexa‚Üífirecrawl‚ÜíplainHTTP‚Üíbrowserbase, heading-tree chunking (768-token len/4 ceiling), hybrid BM25 + potion-base-8M embeddings via `uv run --with model2vec` subprocess, gzipped-JSON page cache with 24h TTL, `¬ß2.3#hash` cites (anchor.Of Crockford base32), guards in plugin/hooks/web_guards.py.

**Wire shapes (live-verified with real keys; keys in gitignored cc-context/.env):** keyless jina 401s in production ("bad network reputation (AS7018)") ‚Äî the keyless 20 RPM tier is dead. jina's `data.warning` is a MULTI-CLASS channel, not an error channel: cache-snapshot notices are benign, "This page maybe requiring CAPTCHA" is a challenge; real jina failures ride the HTTP status. browserbase `/v1/fetch` puts markdown in `content` (not `markdown`), target status in `statusCode`, and supplies **no title**; BROWSERBASE_PROJECT_ID is not required. exa `includeHtmlTags` does yield tagged HTML. firecrawl v2 `data.markdown`/`data.metadata.title` correct.

**Challenge detection (v0.9.1) ‚Äî a signal hierarchy, not a substring scan.** Order: (1) ORIGIN headers `cf-mitigated: challenge` / `x-datadome: protected` ‚Äî only plainHTTP passes non-nil headers, since a hosted tier's `resp.Header` belongs to the *service*, not the origin; (2) title markers; (3) boundary-anchored body markers ‚Äî a marker must not be preceded by `[a-z0-9]`; (4) cleanMarkdown-only, body ‚â§4096B: an interstitial-phrase scan.

Three live-measured traps, each of which a plausible fix gets wrong:
- **Bare `strings.Contains` false-positives on benign 200s**: ticketmaster's i18n JSON `"‚Ä¶title":"Just a Moment"`, nowsecure.nl's embedded Turnstile `<script src=challenges.cloudflare.com>`, wikipedia's `250px-Captchacat.png` matching `px-captcha`. So: `challenges.cloudflare.com` is the *widget host* and must never be a marker; generic phrases are title-scoped; markers are anchored.
- **The 200-trap**: keyed jina relays a Cloudflare interstitial as HTTP 200 whose *content* matches no marker ‚Äî the signal is only in `data.title` + `data.warning`. Ignoring the warning when content is present (the v0.8.1 fix) reintroduces this.
- **Restoring `just a moment` as a BODY marker is a no-op** (measured TP=0): `Extract()` strips `<title>`, so an interstitial's body text never contains it. Only rendered-interstitial phrases ("verifying you are human", "please enable js and disable any ad blocker", ‚Ä¶) move TP, at FP=0 across a benign corpus incl. Cloudflare's own Turnstile docs. Keep the ‚â§4096B cap: it bounds the FPs the corpus can't sample (a long article *quoting* a challenge page). Loose tokens `_px`/`datadome` are ALSO ceiling-gated ‚Äî unbounded, a 50KB article about DataDome flags as a challenge.

**Escalation is proven live** (`TestLiveCascadeEscalatesToBrowserbase`): `errStealthRequired` does NOT short-circuit ‚Äî it sets a flag and the loop continues, so browserbase runs only after every tier fails. With a live JINA_API_KEY jina simply *succeeds* on protected sites, so the test must zero every key and restore only `BROWSERBASE_API_KEY`. Recorded order `[jina, http, browserbase]`; pass on `Tier==TierBrowserbase` OR `errors.Is(err, ErrBlocked)` (ErrBlocked is browserbase-exclusive).

`ErrGone`/`ErrAuthRequired` must early-return from the stealth block: they previously survived only via a leaky `%w` on the final `errors.Join`, so "fixing" that join to `%v` alone silently breaks ErrGone propagation.

Known residual: a target serving HTTP 200 with a >4096B interstitial (indeed.com's 7191B "Security Check") is still cached as content ‚Äî no phrase matches it at any length.

2026-07-10: the four webnav-spike gaps shipped ([[ccx-web-fixes-2026-07]]) ‚Äî plainHTTP now routes by Content-Type (markdown/plain ‚Üí Markdown field, PDF ‚Üí liteparse), `--section` resolves printed numbers, `--offset` pages fixed-stride, budget+offset for OpWebRead moved into web.Run (render.Finalize passes it through).

Related: [[cc-context-architecture]], [[ccx-exec-monty-notarization]].
